title: RuntimeDesignIntent
description: Schema for Design Intents generated from runtime signals. A runtime-generated DI is structurally identical to a human-submitted DI but includes provenance metadata indicating machine origin. The policy engine, panels, and manifest system process these identically to human-submitted DIs.

di_id.description: Unique identifier for this Design Intent.

di_version.description: Semantic version of the DI schema used.

origin.description: Provenance marker. Always 'runtime-signal' for machine-generated DIs.

signal_ids.description: Runtime signal IDs that contributed to this DI. References runtime-signal.schema.json signal_id values.

affected_component.description: Primary affected service, module, or infrastructure component. Must match a component registered in governance/policy/component-registry.yaml.

affected_components_secondary.description: Additional components affected by blast radius.

severity.description: Severity as determined by the highest-severity contributing signal, possibly reclassified per governance/policy/severity-reclassification.yaml.

category.description: Primary classification inherited from the triggering signal. Must match runtime-signal.schema.json category values.

priority_label.description: Human-readable priority label derived from priority score. P0: 80-100, P1: 60-79, P2: 40-59, P3: 20-39, P4: 0-19.

priority_factors.description: Breakdown of the priority calculation factors.

recurrence_penalty.description: 0 if first occurrence; +20 per recurrence in last 30 days, max 100.

time_decay_bonus.description: Signals older than 1 hour lose 5 points per hour, floor 0.

proposed_panel.description: Primary panel to execute for this DI. Determined by signal category via governance/policy/signal-panel-mapping.yaml.

remediation_class.description: Proposed remediation approach. auto_remediate: system can fix without human intervention. panel_review: panel must evaluate before action. human_escalation: requires human decision-maker.

correlation_group_id.description: If this DI was correlated with other signals, the group identifier. Null if no correlation.

recurrence_of.description: If this DI represents a recurrence of a previously closed DI, the closed DI's ID. Null if first occurrence.

requires_human_review.description: Whether this DI requires human review before any action is taken. True for compliance_violation category, severity >= critical, or when correlation engine flags similarity.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this DI was generated.

generator_version.description: Version of the DI generator that produced this intent.

occurrence_count.description: Total number of contributing signals (including deduplicated).

duration_seconds.description: Time span from first to last detection.

impact_assessment.description: Assessment of the impact of the anomaly.

user_impact.description: Description of impact on end users.

sla_impact.description: Which SLAs are affected and to what degree.

root_cause_hypothesis.description: Machine-generated provisional root cause analysis. Explicitly labeled as provisional. Based on signal metrics, historical patterns, and dependency graph traversal.

evidence.description: Evidence collected to support the root cause hypothesis and priority calculation.

constraints.description: Constraints on remediation (e.g., change freeze windows, compliance requirements, dependency constraints).

correlation_method.description: Which correlation criterion matched, if any.

correlated_di_id.description: Existing DI that this signal was correlated with, if any.

similarity_score.description: Cosine similarity score if root_cause_similarity method was used.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of this DI.
